title: Normalize typed inspector value formatting in native content and foundation workspaces
summary: Replace remaining ad hoc native inspector value strings with shared typed formatting so renderer, presentation, group, type, and empty-state truth stay consistent across the macOS app.
acceptance_criteria: Remaining raw inspector values in the touched content and foundation detail surfaces move to shared typed formatters or shared placeholders.
The same native concept uses one localized wording across summary cards, detail rows, and inspector pills.
The slice preserves current inspector behavior while improving truthful native wording and empty-state handling.
Ticket validation, patch hygiene, repo contract validation, and a targeted HumbleStudioMac debug build pass for the slice.

evidence.note: HS-0018 ticket schema validated after normalizing shared typed inspector value formatting.
Patch hygiene passed after replacing remaining ad hoc native inspector values with shared typed formatters and placeholders.
Repo contract validation passed after extending StudioStrings and the string catalog for the native formatting pass.

evidence.path: xcodebuild -project HumbleStudio.xcodeproj -scheme HumbleStudioMac -configuration Debug CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
evidence.note: Native macOS build succeeded after normalizing inspector renderer, presentation, group, type, and empty-state values.

trigger.source_ref: Top 10 re-rank after HS-0017: content and foundation inspectors still leak raw renderer, presentation, group, kind, and empty placeholder values instead of shared typed native wording.

commands: xcodebuild -project HumbleStudio.xcodeproj -scheme HumbleStudioMac -configuration Debug CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
agent_notes: Prefer extending StudioStrings with shared formatter helpers instead of scattering new capitalization logic in views.
Keep root checkout clean after lane claim and release.
